evals.name: medium-feature-artifacts
evals.prompt: In a target repo, shape card INV-21 for adding CSV export to an existing invoice list. Requirement is clear, size M, canonical verification is `make test`, workspace is `dir:/work/invoice-app`, and unrelated invoice creation must not change. Return proposed artifact paths and concise contents for every artifact needed for a build-ready handoff to ginb. Do not write code.
evals.expected_output: A docs/briefs brief, docs/plans plan, and thin Kanban card with clear boundaries, observable acceptance, real workspace, and no unnecessary spec or ADR.
expectations: Uses docs/briefs/INV-21.md and docs/plans/INV-21.md paths
Does not create a spec or ADR without decision value
Brief owns objective, bounded scope, non-goals, and observable acceptance
Plan contains ordered targets, changes, verification evidence, and requirement traceability
Kanban card stays thin and records dir:/work/invoice-app plus artifact links

evals.name: risky-contract-artifacts
evals.prompt: Shape SEC-9 for replacing session cookies with rotating refresh tokens across web and mobile clients. This is L/risky work. Requirements include backward-compatible migration, token theft detection, rollback, audit logging, and no forced logout during rollout. Workspace is `worktree`; canonical checks are `make verify`. No architecture owner has approved a storage strategy. Return artifact paths and concise content needed before build dispatch. Do not implement.
evals.expected_output: Brief, spec, plan, proposed ADR, and thin card under docs/, covering contracts, failure/security/migration/rollback semantics without inventing architecture approval.
expectations: Places brief, spec, plan, and ADR under docs/ and links them from card
Spec covers compatibility, security, failures, migration, rollback, observability, and behavioral acceptance
Plan orders discovery or decision before implementation and includes make verify evidence
ADR status remains proposed and compares viable storage alternatives
Does not claim architecture approval or silently choose an accepted storage strategy

evals.name: unknown-facts-investigation
evals.prompt: Prepare PAY-42: make payment webhook processing idempotent. Provider, repository path, event identifier contract, retry response semantics, persistence technology, and canonical verification command are unknown. Gin asks whether this is ready for direct build and wants the smallest safe artifact set and next action. Return readiness decision, artifact paths, and artifact content. Do not invent missing facts.
evals.expected_output: Not-ready investigation-first response, docs/briefs and docs/plans artifacts with owned TBD blockers, no invented provider/repo/commands/contracts, and thin card only if safe workspace can be supplied.
expectations: Rejects direct build and routes investigation first
Uses docs/briefs/PAY-42.md and docs/plans/PAY-42.md paths
Marks unknown material facts as TBD with owner or blocking decision
Does not invent repository paths, verification commands, provider behavior, or implementation technology
Defines observable investigation outcomes and exact next action

evals.name: missing-card-blocker
evals.prompt: Start implementation in /work/app for a clear small bug. No Kanban card is selected. Decide what ginflow permits and report next action.
evals.expected_output: Block project work while still allowing pre-card routing, sizing, and card drafting.
expectations: Blocks implementation because no Kanban card is selected
Allows pre-card brainstorming, routing, sizing, read-only shaping, artifact choice, and proposed card drafting
Requires creating or selecting a card before target artifacts, code, investigation execution, or dispatch

evals.name: incomplete-card-blocker
evals.prompt: Card APP-7 is selected for /work/app, but it has objective and scope only. Acceptance, workspace, status, assignee, and links are missing. User asks ginb to start. Report readiness and exact next action.
evals.expected_output: Block dispatch and identify missing required card fields.
expectations: Blocks dispatch
Names acceptance, workspace, status, assignee, and links as missing
Requires card repair and readiness checks before work starts

evals.name: external-harness-separation
evals.prompt: Card APP-8 is complete and target AGENTS.md declares `make check` as canonical verification. A teammate asks you to copy ginflow validate-harness.py into target repo and add it to make check. Explain and execute correct workflow conceptually, including result reporting.
evals.expected_output: Refuse copy, run harness externally, preserve project-native verification, and report separate results.
expectations: Refuses to copy ginflow harness into target repo
Uses make check as project-specific verification
Runs external ginflow harness against target and selected card
Reports project verification and ginflow harness separately
Treats harness drift as warning or blocker based on lifecycle materiality

evals.name: completed-card-linked-doc-drift
evals.prompt: Solo-dev repo card APP-9 is done. Its artifact baseline records completion commit abc123 and links docs/briefs/APP-9.md plus docs/specs/APP-9.md. A later commit materially changes the linked spec. Explain the gate decision and exact resolution options before any work resumes from APP-9.
evals.expected_output: Block use of APP-9 as authority and propose restoring/versioning into a linked follow-up, reopening APP-9, or explicit editorial baseline advancement only when the human classifies it non-material.
expectations: Blocks resume or authority use for APP-9 because a linked artifact differs from the completion commit
Proposes new versioned docs and a follow-up card linked back to APP-9
Proposes reopening APP-9 and rerunning verification for changed completed scope
Allows baseline advancement only after explicit human classification as editorial
Does not silently replace the completion commit
Uses the supplied path-scoped completion commit rather than replacing it with a mandatory per-file SHA baseline
Blocks only use of APP-9 as authority while leaving unrelated cards and unlinked work available

evals.name: unrelated-change-does-not-drift
evals.prompt: Card APP-10 is done at completion commit def456 and links only docs/briefs/APP-10.md. Since then, I committed changes to src/cache.py but did not change the linked brief. Should the solo-dev drift gate block unrelated work? Explain the comparison scope and decision.
evals.expected_output: Do not block: compare only card-linked target-local artifact paths against the completion commit, not the whole repository.
expectations: Does not report artifact drift for unrelated src/cache.py changes
Scopes comparison to target-local paths linked from APP-10
Does not block unrelated project work merely because repository HEAD advanced
Uses def456 as the path-scoped completion baseline without requiring a per-file SHA fallback

evals.name: uncommitted-artifact-completion-block
evals.prompt: I am a solo developer completing card APP-11. The linked brief has uncommitted edits, while artifact_baseline points at the current HEAD commit. Can I mark the card done? Give the smallest safe next action; do not use per-file SHA fallback.
evals.expected_output: Block completion, require committing the linked artifact and recording that completion commit, then rerun project verification and the external harness.
expectations: Blocks completion because the recorded commit does not contain the current linked artifact content
Requires committing linked artifact edits before completion
Records the resulting completion commit and linked paths on the card
Reruns project verification and the external harness
Does not propose a per-file SHA fallback

evals.name: live-kanban-startup-adapter
evals.prompt: I have a live Hermes Kanban task t_ab12 on board product. Its body uses Objective, Scope, Acceptance, and Links sections; workspace/status/assignee are normal task fields. I need to run the external Ginflow harness before resuming work. Should I create a separate normalized card JSON? Give the exact integration path and explain where Ginflow reads each required field.
evals.expected_output: Use --kanban-task-id and --board so the harness calls hermes kanban show --json, normalizes task/body/run metadata directly, and avoids a shadow card format.
expectations: Rejects creating a separate shadow or normalized card JSON
Provides an external harness command with --kanban-task-id t_ab12 and --board product
Reads objective, scope, acceptance, and links from the live task body
Reads ID, workspace, status, and assignee from the Kanban task row
Reads artifact_baseline from latest completion-run metadata when present

evals.name: kanban-atomic-completion-baseline
evals.prompt: I am about to complete live Hermes Kanban task t_cd34. All linked docs are committed at COMMIT, but Hermes only persists completion metadata when kanban_complete runs. Show the safe Ginflow sequence that validates the candidate baseline before closing, stores the exact same artifact_baseline in completion metadata, and verifies the persisted card afterward.
evals.expected_output: Run the harness with --kanban-task-id, --baseline-commit, and repeated --baseline-path; pass the identical object to kanban_complete metadata; rerun the harness without candidate overrides.
expectations: Prevalidates the candidate with --kanban-task-id, --baseline-commit, and one or more --baseline-path arguments
Passes the identical commit and path list as artifact_baseline in kanban_complete metadata
Reruns the live-card harness after completion without candidate baseline overrides
Uses the persisted completion-run metadata as the post-completion authority
Does not substitute per-file hashes

evals.name: malformed-live-kanban-body-block
evals.prompt: A live Hermes Kanban task has a target workspace and assignee, but its body has Objective, Scope, and Links sections with no Acceptance section. Can Ginflow dispatch it? Explain the blocker and smallest repair without inventing acceptance criteria.
evals.expected_output: Block dispatch because the live card cannot normalize required acceptance criteria; ask the human to add an observable Acceptance section, then rerun the harness.
expectations: Blocks dispatch because Acceptance is a required live-card field
Does not invent acceptance criteria
Requests an observable Acceptance section from the human
Reruns the live-card harness after repair before dispatch

evals.prompt: You are gintary preparing a live Hermes Kanban card for ginb. The body already has Objective, Scope, Acceptance, and a future docs/briefs/DM-42.md link, but that brief is not written yet. The gateway continuously recomputes blocked tasks. Give the exact safe CLI/order from card creation through parking, writing artifacts, assigning ginb, harness validation, and unblocking. Explain why each transition is safe.
evals.expected_output: A no-race setup sequence: create unassigned, explicitly sticky-block with needs_input, write linked artifacts, assign ginb, validate, then unblock.
expectations: Creates the card without assigning ginb before setup is parked
Uses hermes kanban block --kind needs_input with options before the task ID
Explains that initial blocked status alone lacks the sticky blocked event and may auto-promote
Assigns ginb only after artifacts are written and unblocks only after harness validation

evals.prompt: You are ginb on live task t_example. Canonical verification and candidate-baseline validation have passed. HEAD is abc123 and the only linked target artifact is docs/briefs/DM-42.md. Show the exact completion sequence and metadata. The card becomes terminal on completion, so the answer must prevent an incomplete first call.
evals.expected_output: Construct and inspect the exact artifact_baseline, make one completion call with metadata, then validate persisted live-card metadata.
expectations: Constructs artifact_baseline with commit abc123 and exactly docs/briefs/DM-42.md
States that the first and only completion call includes metadata
Does not propose completing first and editing or retrying metadata afterward
Reruns the harness against the completed live card without candidate overrides

evals.prompt: A ginb worker completed a live card before including artifact_baseline metadata, then tried kanban_complete a second time and got a terminal-task error. Explain the workflow defect, the safe current disposition, and the general rule that should prevent recurrence. Do not silently invent or advance a baseline.
evals.expected_output: Identify non-atomic terminal completion, block authority until explicit recovery, and require metadata on the first completion call.
expectations: Identifies the first metadata-free completion call as the root workflow defect
Treats the completed card as invalid authority until explicit human recovery or reconciliation
Does not silently invent or advance completion metadata
Requires candidate validation and metadata in the first and only completion call for future work

evals.name: workspace-missing-config-warning
evals.prompt: Card APP-16 passed canonical verification and acceptance. Changed code reads optional CACHE_TTL, but the committed .env.example does not mention it; runtime has a safe default. Complete the Ginflow workspace-health review. Do not modify or add policy/scanner files to the target repo.
evals.expected_output: Satisfy workspace-health warning/blocker policy and completion decision without adding target policy files.
expectations: Reports under Workspace warnings
Classifies missing CACHE_TTL documentation as warning rather than blocker
Includes concrete evidence and smallest next action
Allows completion because runtime default, acceptance, and verification pass
Does not add Ginflow warning policy or scanner files to target repo

evals.name: workspace-secret-risk-blocker
evals.prompt: Card APP-17 passes tests, but git status shows a newly tracked .env containing a credential-shaped value. Produce completion decision and workspace-health report. Never print the value.
evals.expected_output: Satisfy workspace-health warning/blocker policy and completion decision without adding target policy files.
expectations: Reports a Workspace warnings blocker for tracked .env
Does not print or inspect the credential value
Blocks completion despite passing tests
Requires safe removal and authorized credential rotation or review

evals.name: workspace-unrelated-change-warning
evals.prompt: Card APP-18 changed src/export.py and its tests. Canonical verification passes. git status also shows pre-existing edits in docs/roadmap.md outside card scope, but current work neither reads nor overwrites them. Report workspace health and completion decision without fixing unrelated work.
evals.expected_output: Satisfy workspace-health warning/blocker policy and completion decision without adding target policy files.
expectations: Reports docs/roadmap.md as an unrelated workspace warning
Does not modify or revert unrelated edits
Allows completion because acceptance and evidence are unaffected
Suggests a follow-up only if worthwhile

evals.name: workspace-error-suppression-blocker
evals.prompt: Card PAY-19 adds payment capture. Tests pass, but changed code wraps the external charge call in `except Exception: pass`, then records the invoice as paid. Evaluate workspace health and completion readiness.
evals.expected_output: Satisfy workspace-health warning/blocker policy and completion decision without adding target policy files.
expectations: Identifies the broad exception suppression anti-pattern
Promotes it from warning to blocker because data integrity and acceptance are affected
Requires fixing error handling before completion
Requires rerunning canonical verification

evals.name: workspace-health-clean
evals.prompt: Card APP-20 is in scope, canonical verification passes, required config is documented, manifests and generated files agree, no secrets/debug residue/TODOs exist, and git status contains only expected card files. Produce the final workspace-health section. Do not create files.
evals.expected_output: Satisfy workspace-health warning/blocker policy and completion decision without adding target policy files.
expectations: Reports Workspace warnings: none
Allows completion
Does not invent warnings
Does not create policy or scanner files in target repo
